rulings.Case Title: KEITH DIETERICH ET AL VS. GRIFFITH VERMONT CENTER PROPERTIES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Griffith Vermont Center Properties, Llc Notice Of Motion To Stay Litigation, Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 26, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT GRIFFITH VERMONT CENTER PROPERTIES, LLC'S NOTICE OF MOTION TO STAY LITIGATION, COMPEL ARBITRATION is GRANTED in part. All the disputes between the moving defendants and unit 3 and 5 tenants are to proceed in arbitration, except for the CAM disputes alleged by all plaintiffs against all defendants (e.g. 9th, 10th and 12th causes of action). This part of the litigation will proceed in this action along with all other remaining issues. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NAIFEH AZAR VS. PABLO E. RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 26, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANTS PABLO RODRIGUEZ, MARTHA RODRIGUEZ, EMMA VANEGAS AND EMELINA SANCHEZ DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Request for Judicial Notice of the Lease is DENIED. Request for Judicial Notice is otherwise GRANTED; and as to the Rent Board file only to the extent that the Court will take judicial notice of its existence of the, but not its contents. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to join owners of the property. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. The complaint is sufficiently alleged, veracity of the allegations cannot be tested on a demurrer. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
